disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Instantaneous onset at peak exertion with a discrete palpable cramping knot that eases on stretch — not gradual diffuse swelling
trigger_question_examples: Did it come on instantly at the hardest part of the run, and is it a discrete tight knot that eases when you stretch?
Is it one hard band, or is the whole calf swollen and tight?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: It hit instantly at the end of the run — it's a hard knot that eases briefly when I stretch, not the whole leg swelling.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: No whole-leg swelling/redness/warmth, no calf tenderness persisting when relaxed, no risk factors for DVT (no immobility, surgery, prior VTE, long-haul travel)
trigger_question_examples: Is the whole leg swollen, red or warm, and does it stay tender when the muscle is relaxed?
Any recent surgery, long flights, immobility or previous clots?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No, the whole leg isn't swollen or red, and when the cramp lets go it's just sore, not constantly tender. No surgery, flights or past clots.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: No pain out of proportion / tense woody whole-compartment swelling, no numbness/paraesthesia, foot warm with normal movement (excludes compartment syndrome/ischaemia)
trigger_question_examples: Is the pain far worse than expected with a tensely swollen calf, or any numbness/tingling or a cold pale foot?
Can you feel and move your toes, and is the foot a normal colour and warmth?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No, once the cramp eases it's manageable, the calf isn't tensely swollen, and my foot is warm with normal feeling and movement.

trigger_question_examples: Has your urine gone dark, like tea or cola?
Any markedly reduced or dark urine since the run?
